_comment: Declarative E2E fixture dataset. Every agent name starts with 'e2e-test-' so the Playwright suite can discriminate its own fixtures amid any other data in the shared dev DB. session_ids are derived deterministically in seed.py via uuid5(NAMESPACE_FLIGHTDECK, 'flightdeck-e2e/<agent_name>/<role>'), so each seed run addresses the same session rows and idempotency is a per-session HTTP GET.

session_roles._comment: Timeline offsets drive the UI state each fixture tests. Offsets are seconds relative to seed-time. 'aged-closed' and 'stale' require a post-seed SQL backdate because the worker stamps last_seen_at = NOW() regardless of event payload timestamp; see seed.py :: backdate_sessions. 'phase4_extras' (when present) is a list of additional event-shape strings the seeder emits on top of the base session_start / pre_call / post_call / tool_call / session_end timeline. Recognised values: 'embeddings' (no-content), 'embeddings_with_content_string' (has_content=true, single-string input), 'embeddings_with_content_list' (has_content=true, list-of-strings input), 'streaming_post_call', 'streaming_post_call_aborted', 'llm_error_rate_limit', 'llm_error_context_overflow', 'llm_error_authentication', 'llm_error_timeout', 'policy_warn', 'policy_degrade', 'policy_block', 'mcp_tool_list', 'mcp_tool_call', 'mcp_tool_call_failed' (mcp_tool_call with structured payload.error — MCPErrorIndicator anchor), 'mcp_resource_list', 'mcp_resource_read', 'mcp_prompt_list', 'mcp_prompt_get'.

fresh-active.purpose: T1 LIVE/RECENT bucket; T5 visible in swimlane; T14 embeddings drawer row -- four variants (no-content, captured-string, captured-list-with-content, captured-list-no-content) covering EmbeddingsContentViewer's render branches; T15 happy-path streaming post_call

recent-closed.purpose: T1 RECENT bucket; T5 closed-state badge; T16 carries one llm_error event so the session row's red error-indicator dot appears alongside the closed badge

aged-closed.purpose: T5 anchor: started 28h ago, ended 27h ago. Must NOT appear in 24h swimlane circles (server filters /v1/sessions by started_at AT fleet.ts::SWIMLANE_LOOKBACK_MS=24h) but MUST appear in expanded drawer via loadExpandedSessions (which fetches with no from/to bound). Plan called for 23h/22h but those values sit INSIDE the 24h window; 28h/27h gives a safe margin either side of the boundary. Requires post-seed SQL backdate AND force_state='closed'.

stale.purpose: T1 non-active state. 3h gap puts it past both the 2-min stale and 10-min lost reconciler thresholds (see workers/internal/writer/postgres.go:543). seed.py pins state='lost' deterministically (matches what the reconciler would set on its next pass) so a freshly-seeded stack is test-stable without waiting for a reconciler tick. Requires post-seed SQL backdate + force_state.

error-active.purpose: T16 anchor: a single live session that emits four distinct llm_error taxonomy values (rate_limit, context_overflow, authentication, timeout) plus an aborted streaming post_call. Drives the ERROR TYPE facet on Investigate (4 distinct pills) and the session-row red dot tooltip (4 distinct values listed). Coding agent only -- the other agents stay clean so T16 can assert the dot is selectively applied.

ancient-only.purpose: T5b anchor: agent whose only sessions are >7 days old, beyond the API's pre-V-DRAWER 7-day default that windowed the expanded swimlane drawer. 9 days started, 8 days ended. Confirms the V-DRAWER fix surfaces these sessions in the drawer (was a dead-end pre-fix). Used only on the dedicated ancient-agent fixture so the canonical pollution stays bounded. Requires post-seed SQL backdate AND force_state='closed' (worker stamps NOW() on every column write).

policy-active.purpose: T17 anchor: live session emitting one of each policy enforcement event type (policy_warn, policy_degrade, policy_block) so the drawer renders all three distinct badges/icons, the Investigate POLICY facet shows three pills with count=1 each, the session-row dot is severity-ranked to red (block > degrade > warn), and the multi-policy expanded grid asserts on from_model/to_model/intended_model. Coding agent only -- the other agents stay clean so T17 can assert the dot is selectively applied.

claude-subagent.purpose: T28 anchor — Claude Code Task subagent. Child session_start carries agent_role='Explore' and parent_session_id pointing back at a Claude Code parent. T28 verifies the child swimlane row + relationship pill + parent-link surface. ``parent_role`` references which parent agent's session is referenced; resolved by seed.py to the concrete UUID.
claude-subagent.captured_input: Investigate the auth module and surface every place that calls AuthMiddleware.
claude-subagent.captured_output: Found 4 callers in src/api/auth.py; the entry point is server.go:142.

crewai-researcher.purpose: T29 anchor — CrewAI Researcher child. Pairs with crewai-writer under the same crewai-parent fixture so the parent's relationship pill renders '→ 2'.
crewai-researcher.captured_input: Gather sources for the quarterly report on Q3 token usage.
crewai-researcher.captured_output: Pulled 12 sources from internal Grafana dashboards + 3 external benchmarks.

crewai-writer.purpose: T29 anchor — CrewAI Writer child. See crewai-researcher comment.
crewai-writer.captured_input: Draft the Q3 token usage section using the Researcher's notes.
crewai-writer.captured_output: Drafted three paragraphs covering the 28% MoM growth + the spike on May 1.

langgraph-research-node.purpose: T30 anchor — LangGraph agent-bearing node 'research_node'. Pairs with langgraph-writer-node under the same langgraph-parent.
langgraph-research-node.captured_input: Research the latency spike on the ingest path between 04:00 and 05:00 UTC.
langgraph-research-node.captured_output: Identified a runaway worker on docker-workers-2; CPU saturation 04:14-04:42.

langgraph-writer-node.captured_input: Compose an incident note based on the research_node findings.
langgraph-writer-node.captured_output: Posted incident note INC-2026-148 summarising the worker saturation.

subagent-error.purpose: T40 (L8) anchor — sub-agent that ended in lost state. seed.py emits session_start with parent_session_id + agent_role but NO matching session_end so the worker's state-revival path lands on lost. Drives the red-dot row-level failure cue across Investigate / AgentTable / Swimlane per METHODOLOGY L8.

subagent-overflow-input.purpose: T38 anchor — sub-agent with a >8 KiB incoming_message body. seed.py forces D119 overflow path: incoming_message.has_content=true on the wire and the full body lands in event_content. Drives the click-to-expand fetch via /v1/events/{id}/content.

depth-2-middle.purpose: T35 anchor — middle layer of a depth-2 tree. Itself a child of e2e-test-depth2-grand (the grandparent) AND a parent of depth-2-leaf. Drives the SubAgentsTab combined view.
depth-2-middle.captured_input: Coordinate two sub-tasks across the leaf agent.

depth-2-leaf.purpose: T35 anchor — leaf of a depth-2 tree. Child of depth-2-middle (which is itself a child of e2e-test-depth2-grand). Drives '+N more levels' depth-aware UX cues.

mcp-active.purpose: Phase 5 T25 anchor: live session that connected to two MCP servers (one stdio, one http) and emitted one event of each Phase 5 type (mcp_tool_list, mcp_tool_call, mcp_resource_list, mcp_resource_read, mcp_prompt_list, mcp_prompt_get) plus one mcp_tool_call_failed (anchors T25-16 / D-MCP-FAIL — MCPErrorIndicator on a structured-error MCP row). The mcp_tool_call additionally has capture_prompts=True so the dashboard's MCPEventDetails shows arguments + result. context.mcp_servers carries both server fingerprints so the session-drawer MCP SERVERS panel and the Investigate MCP SERVER facet aggregation both have data to render. Sensor agent only — Python sensor emits all six per Phase 5 D1; the Claude Code plugin emits MCP_TOOL_CALL only.

concurrent-run-a.purpose: T52 anchor — first of two concurrent runs under the same agent_id. Simulates a multi-pod K8s deployment with FLIGHTDECK_HOSTNAME collapsed across pods: both pods register the same agent identity and emit independent session_ids whose start/end intervals overlap. Drives the RunBracket offset (top anchor).

concurrent-run-b.purpose: T52 anchor — second of two concurrent runs under the same agent_id. Starts 100s after concurrent-run-a, both still active, so the brackets overlap on the timeline and stagger to opposite vertical anchors.

fresh-closed-in-window.purpose: RunBracket end-square anchor — closed run with BOTH started_at and ended_at inside the swimlane's default 1-minute window so the end-square glyph (■) has a position to render at. The other ``recent-closed`` role is older (started -10min), outside the 1-minute window; without this fixture no closed session's end-square ever materialises on the live page. seed.py forward-dates this row on every keep-alive cycle so it stays in window.

fresh-subagent-in-window.purpose: SubAgentConnector in-window anchor — sub-agent session_start inside the swimlane's default 1-minute window so the connector overlay has a parent-child pair whose first events are both in the visible domain. The other sub-agent fixtures (claude-subagent, crewai-*, langgraph-*, depth2-*, subagent-*) all started >120s ago so their session_start events fall outside the 1-minute window and the connector geometry skips them. seed.py forward-dates this row on every keep-alive cycle so it stays in window.
fresh-subagent-in-window.captured_input: Connector anchor — fresh sub-agent emitting in-window events.
fresh-subagent-in-window.captured_output: Sub-agent emitted three tool_call events; parent's spawn event circle anchored the connector.

connector-parent-fresh.purpose: Dedicated parent session for the SubAgentConnector in-window anchor. seed.py keeps this session's started_at and a fresh tool_call event in window on every keep-alive cycle so the connector geometry pass finds an in-window event on the parent endpoint. Decoupled from e2e-test-coding-agent's fresh-active session (which T14/T15/T16/T18 consume) so the keep-alive's connector-anchor refresh doesn't perturb those tests' specific event-count / event-ordering assertions.

agents._comment: T28 child agent. agent_role 'Explore' lives on each session; D126 § 2 derives a distinct agent_id because the agent_role joins the 6-tuple input.
T29 parent — Crew kickoff. Children (crewai-researcher / crewai-writer) reference this agent's fresh-active session as their parent_session_id.
T30 parent — LangGraph runner. Agent-bearing node children point at this agent's fresh-active session.
T40 (L8) anchor — sub-agent whose state ends in lost. seed.py force_state='lost' bakes the row state.
T38 anchor — sub-agent with a >8 KiB incoming_message body driving the D119 overflow fetch path.
T35 grandparent — top of the depth-2 tree.

T52 anchor — single agent_id with two overlapping active sessions (simulates multi-pod K8s with FLIGHTDECK_HOSTNAME collapsed across pods). The swimlane renders two start brackets on the agent's row at staggered top/bottom anchors per RunBracket's bracketAnchors() rule.

Dedicated parent agent for the SubAgentConnector in-window anchor. Decoupled from e2e-test-coding-agent so the keep-alive's connector-anchor refresh emissions don't perturb T14/T15/T16/T18's specific event-ordering assertions on coding-agent's fresh-active session.

SubAgentConnector in-window anchor — sub-agent paired with e2e-test-connector-parent's connector-parent-fresh session so the connector overlay has a parent-child pair with both endpoints inside the swimlane's default 1-minute domain. Keeps the connector overlay rendering at least one Bezier path on the live page without the operator widening the time window.
